title: Exotic Aerated Andalusian Chilli Paella
description: A winey, aerated, adventurous Spanish (Andalusian) dish featuring chilli powder, katsuramuki-cut, low-glycemic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the saffron-infused olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the eggplant and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the chorizo and cook until crispy, about 3 minutes.
Add the chilli powder and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the garlic and onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the tomato and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the white wine and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
Add the paella rice and stir to coat with the broth.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, then add the salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the pan to the oven and cook at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the pan from the oven and use a fork to aerate the paella, creating a crispy crust on the bottom.
Garnish with chopped parsley, grated manchego cheese, and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, if desired.
To serve, place the paella in the center of the table and have everyone dig in!
